comments.author: @ArtFreeman
comments.text: I lived there for two years and enjoyed my stay.

replies.author: @AtlasPlanetOfficial
replies.text: That’s wonderful to hear! 😊 Two years is enough time to truly experience a place beyond the typical tourist perspective. We’re glad Singapore left you with such positive memories. Thanks for sharing!

comments.author: @spiznasty
comments.text: Wanted to go to SG for a long time and finally got the chance to go this past Feb. Such a beautiful Country / City and wished I could've stayed there forever instead of returning home.

comments.author: @catmatism
comments.text: The thumbnail... We Singaporeans are not like that. 😂 We are very reserved, dressed humbly.

replies.text: We definitely know Singapore is known for its respectful and modest culture.

comments.author: @brianjc3533
comments.text: Gosh, this was good. Then you mentioned rising sea levels. 😂

comments.author: @Phillibe
comments.text:
I use to be in the Australian navy late 80s to late 90s..
I was told back in the 70s and before... singapore was very poor.. lot of crime... Maylasia did not want to merge then did then left
So they knew how life can be so changed and become strict in laws and lifestyle.. now a wealthy country.. most Singaporians rather this then the past. Locals cant use the Casino... Now singaore dollar is vert strong.
Back in my time i got $1 Aus = $1.22 singaporian dollar..

Now only 83 cents lol for A Aussie dollar...

I really enjoyed my vists there.. must is a haircut as get a head massage.... Singaporians to me are quiet people... not outgoing... keep to themselves...i bet now Mayalasia wish was Singapore lol😊..... Snooker popukar there... Newton circus a food area is awesome if still going.... i would love go back there and drive to Maylasia dream of mine vist both countries Casino..

Singapore is amazing success story

replies.author: @AtlasPlanetOfficial
replies.text: Thank you for sharing these fascinating memories! Singapore's journey from a developing nation to one of the world's most successful cities is truly extraordinary. It's great to hear that your visits left such positive memories, and we hope you'll have the opportunity to experience Singapore again in the future. 😊 Is there a place in Singapore that you still remember most fondly?

replies.author: @HelioTravel
replies.text: What an incredible story—thank you for taking the time to share it! 😊It's fascinating to hear how you've seen Singapore change over the years. It really is one of the world's greatest success stories, and that's one of the reasons so many people love visiting today. We're glad this video brought back those memories for you. If you had the chance to visit Singapore again, what would be the very first place you'd go back to? 🇸🇬✨

comments.author: @HelioTravel
comments.text: This is one of the best Singapore travel videos I've watched! 🇸🇬 Every location is beautifully filmed, making it feel like I was exploring the city myself. The views of Marina Bay Sands, Gardens by the Bay, Jewel Changi Airport, and the lively streets of Singapore are absolutely breathtaking. Your cinematic style, smooth editing, and attention to detail make this travel documentary stand out. Anyone planning a trip to Singapore should definitely watch this video from start to finish. Looking forward to your next travel adventure! 🌏📹✨

replies.author: @AtlasPlanetOfficial
replies.text: Thank you! That truly means a lot to us. Our goal is to create documentaries that let viewers experience each destination beyond the usual tourist highlights. We're so happy you enjoyed exploring Singapore with us, and we hope you'll join us on our next journey as well! ✨
